meaning: adj.要使颈骨折断似的,非常危险的
examples: Jack drove to the hospital at breakneck speed.
The comedy Bringing Up Baby, on the other hand, presents practically non-stop dialogue delivered at breakneck speed.
And you've also faced the breakneck, unrelenting climb of costs for your health care needs.
His firm was growing at breakneck speed, and he was genuinely enjoying the wild ride.
